headline: Flavel, a victim of police violence, tells his story: 'If there hadn't been videos, no one would ever have believed me'
description: On March 16, the former musician went out to order food in Noisiel, in the eastern Paris suburbs. Police violently arrested him, as seen in a video widely shared online. He described to Le Monde the events that led him to file a complaint with the internal affairs agency.

headline: UN force in Lebanon says soldier killed in country's south
Israel backtracks, grants Latin Patriarch access to Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem
Migrant Channel crossings: France-UK negotiations over border funding get bogged down
description: France and the UK are struggling to reach an agreement on a new British funding cycle as London, under pressure from UK public opinion, has demanded greater results from Paris in preventing migrants from crossing the English Channel.

headline: UN Convention grants new protections to 40 species
description: The list includes several new species, such as the snowy owl, the hammerhead shark and the giant otter.

headline: Is it ever a good idea to... wear leather pants?
description: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or do so deliberately.

headline: In wartime Tehran, rare photos capture daily life under bombardment
description: The photographs taken by an Iranian photographer and published by Le Monde offer a unique window into the capital's daily life. With 14 million inhabitants, Tehran has been shrouded in a media blackout since February 28. For his safety, the photographer has requested anonymity.

headline: US to allow a Russian oil tanker to bring relief to Cuba, despite blockade

'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
Israeli writer Etgar Keret wonders if life 'must be reduced to passively reacting to the orders of warlords'
description: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 4/4. Le Monde asked Sinan Antoon (Iraq), Etgar Keret (Israel) and Diane Mazloum (France, Lebanon) for their perspectives on the war.
byline: Etgar Keret,  Diane Mazloum and  Sinan Antoon

headline: In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
description: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

headline: Ukrainians' suffering must not be forgotten amid the war against Iran
Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
Thierry Breton: 'A golden rule limiting the public deficit to 1% of GDP must be enshrined in the French Constitution by 2032'
